div1.entryFree: Philosopher, one versed in moral and intellectual science: Wiv. I, 1, 236. Ado V, 1, 35. Merch. I, 2, 53 (the weeping p.; so Heraclitus of Ephesus was called, in opposition to the laugher Democritus). As III, 2, 33. V, 1, 36. Tim. I, 1, 221. II, 2, 131. Lr. III, 4, 159. Lr. III, 4, 159 Lr. III, 4, 159 The --'s stone, a preparation supposed to have the power of converting any metal into gold, mentioned in a quibbling manner (cf. Stone): I will make him a --'s two stones to me, H4B III, 2, 355. sometime like a p., with two stones moe than's artificial one, Tim. II, 2, 117.
